data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Minimizes decision-making time by reducing the number of choices per page.
data.technical_spec: Simple, clean interfaces with prominent call-to-action elements.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Designs are optimized such that interactive elements are easily clickable.
data.technical_spec: Consistent use of larger, easily accessible buttons.

data.title: The Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Apple.com focuses on visually pleasing design to improve perceived usability.
data.technical_spec: High-quality imagery and balanced color schemes.

data.title: Serial Position Effect
data.description: Arranges key information at the beginning and end of navigation flows.
data.technical_spec: Sequentially structured product pages for effective information delivery.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Leverages user familiarity by maintaining interface consistency.
data.technical_spec: Consistent navigation and interface elements across products.
